Committee.CommitteeName: Joint Committee on the Justice System

RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 305-A, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Cameron Bunting Parker, a Republican, represents Dunklin County, the southern portion of Pemiscot County, and southwestern Butler County (District 150) in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2022.
In addition to her legislative service, Parker is an attorney and has practiced law in Southeast Missouri and Northeast Arkansas for 24 years. She currently has a law practice in Malden, Missouri.
Her past service includes serving on the Campbell School Board and the Women’s Domestic Violence Shelter Board. She is a member of the Daughters of the American Revolution.
A graduate of Risco High School, Parker attended Central Methodist College to play basketball on a collegiate level. Thereafter, she earned a bachelor’s degree in Political Science from the University of Missouri. She obtained her Juris Doctorate from Mississippi College School of Law in Jackson, Mississippi.
Parker resides in Campbell with her husband and two children. The family attends the First Baptist Church.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to the principal place of administration of a trust
Modifies provisions relating to the limitations of an action against a trustee
Establishes provisions relating to electronic estate planning
Provides for MO HealthNet coverage of hearing aids and cochlear implants
Establishes provisions relating to estate planning during an emergency
Modifies provisions relating to civil proceedings
Modifies and establishes provisions relating to estate planning
Repeals the expiration date of funding of basic civil legal services for certain persons
Modifies provisions relating to fees and expenses for an interpreter or translator in certain proceedings
Modifies the "Higher Education Core Curriculum Transfer Act"
Designates August 6th each year as "Chris Sifford Day"
Modifies provisions relating to the dental professions
Modifies provisions relating to Missouri state highway patrol salary increases
Adds endangering the welfare of a child in the first degree to the definition of dangerous felony and modifies the offense of endangering the welfare of a child in the first degree

Provides certain immunity for private contractors, subcontractors, and employees of such contractors and subcontractors acting within the scope of a government contract awarded by the Missouri department of transportation
Establishes provisions relating to nondisclosure agreements in childhood sexual abuse cases
Modifies provisions relating to civil actions for childhood sexual abuse
Establishes a licensure compact for dentists and dental hygienists
Establishes a program for services for certain youth in the custody of the department of social services
Modifies and establishes provisions relating to the protection of children and vulnerable persons
Creates provisions relating to water exportation outside the state
Modifies provisions relating to court administration
Repeals provisions relating to the board of paroles in certain judicial circuits
Proposes a constitutional amendment allowing retrospective laws to be enacted if the laws involve civil childhood sexual abuse claims
